titleStmt.title: Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 617

div.p: Alexander by the grace of God king of Scots to all worthy men of his whole land, greeting: Know that we have quitclaimed to the monks of Coldingham the aid and army which they were accustomed to render to us of old for the twelfth toun of Coldinghamshire, that is for the toun in which the church of Coldingham was founded. Wherefore we will that the aforesaid monks henceforth should not answer for the aid and army which used to be rendered to us from the aforesaid twelfth toun. As witnesses: Master Matthew, chancellor, Walter son of Alan, steward, Ingram de Balliol, William of Lindsay, Thomas de la Haye. At Edinburgh, on the fifth day of June, in the 13th year of our reign.
